title: [v2.0.5-rhel] add github action to add branch prefix to PR title
body:
**Note that this PR targets a non-main branch**

Add a GitHub action to the stable branch to prefix the PR title with the
branch name (i.e., "[v2.0.5-rhel]").  The intention behind is to make
backports, or commits in general, to non-master branches more obvious.
The target branch of a pull request is not easily visible in the GitHub
web interface, and that can lead to unintentionally merging PRs to stable
branches.

Unfortunately, I did not manage to automatically extract and set the
target branch name as the PR prefix.  The action [1] supports a %branch%
macro but that evaluates to the source branch.  Hence, for the time
being, we have to hard-code the branch in the .yml file and update it
once we can auto-set the target branch name.  I opened an issue asking
for help.

Since the action enforces a non-empty body template, the PR body is also
prefixed with a bold note that the PR is targetting a non-main branch.

[1] https://github.com/tzkhan/pr-update-action

Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <rothberg@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *vrothberg* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/v2.0.5-rhel/OWNERS)~~ [vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

@mheon, what do you think?

I am pretty happy with it now. Ideally, we wouldn't have to hard-code the title prefix but I opened an issue for that.
Thanks! I'll prepare a PR for master as well, so future branches inherit it.
